observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
a. Analyze and record the proposed adjusting/correcting entries in the
books to rectify the noted overstatement/understatement of the RPT
and SET Receivables and its related accounts; and
b. Follow the correct accounting entries regarding advance payments
of real property and special education taxes in order to reflect the
correct amount of affected accounts in the financial statements.
b. Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Transfer the deposited collections on the advance payments of RPT
and SET to its appropriate bank accounts and take up in the books
the necessary adjustments; and
b. Henceforth, see to it that the full amount received and collected be
deposited to the appropriate account or accounts to which the
monies in question properly belong.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a. Instruct the members of the MIC to:
1. Include in the physical count all properties owned and controlled
by the LGU, not only those within the LGU premises;
2. Properly accomplish the RCPPE and other necessary reports; and
3. Conduct reconciliation with the accounting records;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher to
adjust the properties below the capitalization threshold and ensure that
all future acquisitions of properties below the capitalization threshold of
₱15,000.00 will be recorded in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006;
and
c. Create an Inventory Committee, who are temporarily relieved of all their
regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or
less, and to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to
the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled
start of inventory-taking activities; and ensure full implementation of
“One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular
2020-006.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Immediately revise the submitted Utilization Report for the 20%
Development Fund of CY 2021 and exercise due diligence in the
preparation of accurate and timely reports. Likewise, refrain from
submission of erroneous and misleading report to COA and DILG;
2. Immediately effect the necessary adjustment on the erroneous
recognition of the on-going infrastructure projects to be made
continuing on the succeeding year as follows:
Advances to Contractor 1,586,284.50
Accounts Payable 15,090,027.20
CIP-Infrastructure Asset 16,676,311.70
b. Direct the Municipal Engineer, Chairman of the Bids and Awards
Committee and other personnel concerned to fast track the
implementation of the planned PPAs to ensure its timely delivery of
services and maximum utilization of the same.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant properly record all funds received by the LGU from the time of
receipt, to the final completion of the project; and the eventual liquidation to
the source agency. Further, ensure faithful representation of the financial
transactions that is complete, neutral and free from material error.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to:
1. Acknowledge all receipt and transfer of funds through issuance of
official receipt and immediately deposit the same within the
prescribed period;
2. Facilitate transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF to the Special Trust
Account of LGU through Debit Memorandum to effect real time
transfer of funds; and
3. Revert back the unexpended portion of the 70% Mitigating Fund-
CO amounting to ₱378,400.00 and ensure that the transfer of the
unexpended LDRRM are only the unutilized portion of the 30%
QRF and 70% Mitigating Fund-MOOE.
b. The Municipal Accountant to:
1. Ensure proper recording of receipt, utilization and transfer of
unexpended funds under the LDRRMF and immediately effect the
necessary adjustment in the TF books as follows;
Cash In Bank – LCCA ₱4,731,045.77
Trust Liability-DRRMF ₱4,731,045.77
2. Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the
balances of unexpended LDRRMF as showed in Annex D of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 for fair presentation and to enhance the
understandability of the users of financial statements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require:
a. The concerned MHO personnel to submit immediately to the audit team,
thru the Accountant, the following documents to establish their claim to
AHDP and SRAs under AO Nos. 35, 36 and 42:
1. Authority for the entitlement of the allowance/ office order/
authority to report for work;
2. Daily Time Records with Biometric printouts/copies of logbook or
Certificate of Attendance in the COVID Facility/Center by their
immediate supervisor during the period of claims;
3. Accomplishment reports indicating the number of COVID patients
attended to in a day;
4. For health workers not directly catering COVID-19 patients, an
explanation as to the scientific basis for the premise that healthcare
workers not directly catering to COVID-19 patients are equally
exposed to COVID-19 and can be considered “in contact with
COVID-19 patients”, and thus should be given the AHDP and SRA.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize Hazard Pay, Other
Personnel Benefits and the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses
Accounts by ₱178,363.64, ₱640,681.79 and ₱855,363.61, respectively, and
the corresponding Subsidy from NGAs in the total amount of
₱1,674,409.01 in the payments of AHDP and SRA to permanent and
contractual employees in the CY 2021 Statement of Financial
Performance.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to immediately submit the pictures of
before and during the construction of the projects, As-Built Plans of the
completed projects and Certification from the end-user that the project is
completed and inspected ; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting
documents for all progress payments of insfrastructure projects and
immediately submit the Nine lacking DVs totaling ₱9,773,953.40.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the
submission of complete supporting documents relative to the implementation
of the TUPAD program and liquidated the same within the reglementary
period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the
Chairman of the BAC and its members to:
a. Submit written justification on the awarding of contracts to JFR
Construction and Venus Engineering and Construction despite non-
disclosure of their on-going projects in the SLCC.
b. Scrutinize carefully the eligibility documents submitted by interested
bidders so that any misrepresentation will be detected and declare as
eligible only those who strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184;
c. Submit written justification on the noted variations of the
contract/project duration between the POW and Contract of
Agreement; and
d. Submit the lacking documents which were not submitted together with
the contracts and justifications on the other noted deficiencies.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer
to intensify the collection efforts of her office and should coordinate with other
LGU officials to devise collection strategies and avail all legal remedies
provided under RA No. 7160 to collect all taxes due to the LGU.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a. Issue an EO creating the M&E Team to assist the GFPS-TWG
Chairperson and MSWD in establishing a gender-responsive M&E
system; and
b. Incorporate in the CY 2023 GAD Plan and Budget, the M&E Team’s
activities to gather the much-needed data and information essential for
establishing and enhancing the LGU GAD policies, programs, and
projects.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Reconcile the discrepancies totaling ₱42,035.98 between the amount
withheld and the amount remitted for the current year;
b. Reconcile the previous years’ outstanding balance of ₱310,520.95;
c. Submit the JEV and supporting documents for the refunds posted in
the GL;
d. Record the Employee Compensation Insurance Premium and all other
premiums and contributions payable to the GSIS as Due to GSIS and
remit the exact amount due to them; and
e. Remit immediately the reconciled balance of Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-
IBIG and PhilHealth accounts as at December 31, 2021.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant and other officials concerned to immediately submit the
disbursement vouchers totaling ₱8,787,687.37, together with their
supporting documents, and cancelled checks, if any, for purposes of review
and audit.

Henceforth, the City Accountant ensure that all disbursement vouchers and
their supporting documents, copies of cancelled checks and other transaction
documents are submitted on a regular and timely basis.

1.14 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to assess and validate the collectability
of the receivables and provide allowance for impairment based on the
results of the assessment; and
b. require a regular review, reconciliation and monitoring of all booked
receivables to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded in the books.
Analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and take
necessary actions to drop them from the books in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

2.13 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require:
a. the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the issued relief goods totaling
₱20,758,962.00 from Food Supplies Expenses to Welfare Goods Expenses
in the CY 2020 Statement of Financial Performance. Henceforth, record
relief goods procured as Welfare Goods for Distribution.
b. the Municipal Treasurer/Property and Supply Custodian to: (a) oblige the
requesting personnel to prepare the RIS for every withdrawal of goods
procured in bulk and (b) prepare and submit the required RSMI together
with the RIS to the Accounting Office for recording purposes.

3.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to submit immediately to the
Audit Team the required Appropriation Ordinances with the Supplemental
Budget/s and the supporting documents as required under Section 121 of R.A.
No. 7160 for the COVID-19 related PPAs amounting to ₱16,373,262.20.
Henceforth, stop implementing Programs, Projects and Activities chargeable
to the LGU fund without approved Appropriation Ordinance.

4.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Development Council (MDC) and the Municipal Planning Development
Coordinator to prepare a detailed Supplemental or amended AIP for COVID-
19 PPAs funded by LDRRM and BGCM amounting to ₱25,324,261.20 and
submit to the Sangguniang Bayan for adoption/approval.

4.9 Also, we recommended and the Sangguniang Bayan, thru the SB Secretary
agreed to ensure that prior to adoption of supplemental budget, updated/
supplemental AIPs contain the specific PPAs and not lumped, to avoid delay
in the implementation and possible questions on the sufficiency of
authorization of the Sanggunian to use the supplemental appropriation for
unspecified PPAs.

5.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct:

c. the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents
enumerated in Appendix F to the Audit Team for further evaluation of the
transactions pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

d. the BAC, through its Secretariat, to immediately post for information
purposes the Notice of Award (NOA), contract, or purchase order in the
GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act
of the procurements made through negotiated mode of procurement under
emergency cases.

6.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to: (a) strictly adhere
to the guidelines on the use of government vehicles by adopting controls
on the issuance of driver’s trip tickets and monitoring the fuel
consumption of the LGU; and (b) require all drivers to completely
accomplish all entries of the trip tickets and monthly report of Fuel
consumption based on the prescribed formats, and to prepare the
Monthly Report of Official Travels as prescribed under COA Circular
No. 77-61.

7.5 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to request an
authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to open a separate bank
account for the DOLE Livelihood (Kabuhayan) Project in compliance
with the MOA.

8.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to:

d. Effect payment of goods and services through checks payable
directly to suppliers or dealers, except in cases where the needed
items/services are extremely necessary or the activities related
thereto are unplanned/unexpected;

e. Discourage the practice of procuring goods and services through
reimbursement basis, unless the disbursements are petty in nature
and may be charged to Petty Cash Fund; and

f. Consider the setting-up of Petty Cash Fund duly supported by an
estimate of expenses which shall be strictly for petty operating
expenses in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

9.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the MSWDO and the members of the LCPC to prepare and submit
the AWFP, LDPC, and LIPC as required under DILG MC No 2012-120
dated July 4, 2012
b. Maximize utilization of the allocated LCPC Fund to ensure full the
promotion of children’s welfare and protection.

We recommended that the LCE direct (1) Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. (2) Acting Municipal General Services Officer, or in his default, the Municipal Treasurer, to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Local Road Inventory and Road Map to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s local road network system and to prepare as well a Report on Local Road Network. (3) Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal General Services Officer or in his default, the Municipal Treasurer, with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects. (4) Municipal Accountant to record the amount of local road networks equivalent to the required transition provision percentage and to disclose the total road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: (1) Not Implemented (2) Not Required Implemented (3) Not Implemented (4) Not Implemented
observations.reason: The LGU failed to conduct physical count and to constitute an Inventory Committee per reference to CY 2019 AAR, Part II No. 1. Required reports were not submitted. The Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to coordinate to prepare Local Road Inventory and Road Map to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s local road network system and to prepare as well a Report on Local Road Network. Said report from the Municipal Engineer was not provided to the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer. The Municipal Accountant was not able to comply with the pertinent recommendation due to the absence of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE oblige: (21) the Municipal Accountant to file a request for write-off of the receivable account balances which are considered dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: No request was made by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE: (30) transfer to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund any subsequent unexpended prior year balance which is already due for reversion. (31) support subsequent disbursements charged to Quick Response Fund with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and the declaration of the state of calamity. (32) transfer the amount of ₱9,421.01 to the Special Trust Fund for DRRMF and to effect the necessary adjusting entry, i. e., a debit to the Government Equity account and a credit to Cash in Bank account in the General Fund for ₱9,421.01, and a debit to Cash in Bank account and a credit to Trust Liability – DRRMF CY 2018 account in the Trust Fund for ₱9,421.01. (33) disclose in its Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of its LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: (30) Implemented (31) Not Implemented (32) Implemented (33) Not Implemented
observations.reason: Support for disbursements charged to Quick Response Fund with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and the declaration of the state of calamity were not provided. Transfer of ₱9,421.01 to Special Trust Fund for DRRMF was implemented. No disclosure was made in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU observe strictly the prescribed minimum employee’s monthly net take home pay under Section 47 of the General Appropriations Act of 2017.
observations.reason: Some LGU employees still had below ₱5,000.00 minimum take home pay as of CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management substantially comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and: (54) require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the local roads; (55) submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System; (56) enjoin all concerned department heads to submit a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation; (57) maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; (58) keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, and render a Report on Local Road Network and recognize impairment on infrastructure assets.
observations.managementAction: (54) Not Implemented (55) Not Implemented (56) Not Implemented (57) Not Implemented (58) Not Implemented
observations.reason: Inventory Committee is yet to be created. Inventory List is incomplete as of CY 2019. No Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network was submitted. No lapsing schedule for computation of depreciation was submitted. No Local Road Inventory and Road Map was maintained; no Property Card maintained; impairment not recognized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (59) draw a journal entry voucher to record the donated equipment to its proper asset accounts; (60) provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements; (61) require the Property and Supply unit to record the other in-kind donations in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM. Henceforth, adhere strictly to COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: (59) Not Implemented (60) Not Implemented (61) Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has yet to record the donated equipment; supporting documents from the Health Office are awaited. No disclosure was made in the Notes to Financial Statements. In-kind donations were not recorded in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to: (62) record the procurement of supplies, accountable forms, fuels, lubricants, drugs and medicines through inventory, accountable forms issuance to end-users, and transfers or other disposal.
observations.reason: The LCE has yet to create a team to conduct the inventory of all supplies, accountable forms, fuels, drugs and medicines. Inventory materials, accountable forms, fuels, drugs not yet properly recorded or disposed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 particularly on fund transfers be strictly adhered. Also, require that supporting documents for the said fund transfer be submitted and project implementation monitored.
observations.reason: Supporting documents for the said fund transfer were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to: (73) maintain in the General Fund a Special Account for the Municipality’s Waterworks System. (74) determine whether the Water System warrants classification as Business Enterprise and maintain special accounts if necessary.
observations.reason: Municipal Treasurer communicated that BLGF advised them that the Municipality’s Water System does not warrant classification as a Business Enterprise. No document was submitted to the Audit Team to prove such advisory. Special Account was not maintained.

observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended, and the Management agreed to:
(a) Facilitate the creation of a functional Inventory Committee to conduct
physical count of properties of the LGU;
(b) Require the Inventory Committee to affix property tags to each property
and equipment during physical count for proper control and monitoring;
(c) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain/update the
PPE Ledger Cards; and
(d) Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain/update
Property Cards and properly accomplish and update the Property
Acknowledgement Receipts.

2.8 We recommended, and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
1. Record and adjust immediately in its respective books all reconciling
items found to be valid.
2. Investigate, analyze and verify the details of unreconciled balance of
₱207,448.45, and record appropriate adjustments duly supported by
documents.

3.5 We recommended, and the Management agreed to require:
1. The Municipal Treasurer to update its Real Property Tax Account
Register regularly and furnish the Municipal Accountant with a certified
list of taxpayers with the stated amount due and collectible for the year.
2. The Municipal Accountant to set up the RPT/SET receivables at the
beginning of the accounting period based on the certified list of taxpayers
due and collectible for the year prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.
3. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their
respective records pertaining to balances of RPT/SET receivables.

4.7 We recommended that management requires the Municipal Accountant to
trace the period or date of acquisition of the assets and compute the
depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as provided under
COA Circular 2003-007 and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries
to update the carrying value of the said assets.

5.7 We recommended that Management dispose all unserviceable properties at
the earliest possible time, pursuant to Section 79 of PD No. 1445, Sections 379-
381 of RA 7160, and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular
No. 89-296.

6.10 We recommended, and the Management agreed to immediately submit the
MOA or Trust Agreement together with Local Sanggunian Resolution
authorizing the LCE to enter into a contract for the fund transfer from DENR
EMB amounting to ₱451,250.00, and ensure that the COA Audit Team is
always furnished a copy of the mentioned documents necessary in the audit of
the receipt and utilization of the Trust Fund, as well as in the evaluation of
project implementation.

7.7 We recommended that the LCE and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Council ensure that there is sufficient and necessary
equipage to address the DRRM Operations of the LGU as prescribed in
NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

8.8 We recommended, and the Management agreed to undertake appropriate
actions to secure the TCT in the name of the Municipality, and a duly
annotated Deed of Donation in favor of the government from the heirs/owners
of the private lot where the projects were constructed or installed, in order to
safeguard the government resources against loss or wastage.

9.11 We recommended, and the Management agreed to require the HRMO to:
(a) Coordinate with the heads of the departments and offices to prepare a
contract incorporating a well-defined job description stating therein the
specific work to be assigned to each JO personnel;
(b) Require each JO personnel to prepare their respective approved DTRs;
and
(c) Submit a copy of Detailed Individual Accomplishment Reports to
facilitate comparison with the job description stated on their contracts,
thereby, ensuring the protection of government funds from unnecessary
expenditures.

10.16 We recommended, and the Management agreed to:
a) instruct the HRMO to require the JO/COS to submit Tax Identification
Number (TIN) and Annual Registration Fee (ARF) before hiring and
renewal of contracts, otherwise, require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to compute, withhold and remit the respective
percentage taxes, as provided in RMC 51-2018 and other pertinent laws,
rules, and regulations; and
b) capacitate the Municipal Accounting Personnel, by requiring them to
attend seminars and trainings related to the latest updates on Tax and BIR
regulations.

11.7 We recommended, and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
a. reconcile the discrepancies totaling ₱7,375.74 between the amount
withheld and the amount remitted for the current year.
b. reconcile the previous years’ outstanding balance of ₱222,046.61.
c. remit immediately the reconciled balance of Due to Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth
and GOCC accounts as of December 31, 2019.

12.9 We recommended, and the Management agreed to require the members of the
GAD Focal Point System to:
1. Immediately revise the GAD Accomplishment Report and provide the
actual result obtained in the implementation of GAD PPAs.
2. Indicate the variances noted between the actual results against the
performance targets, and provide the reasons for such deviation and
factors that hindered the implementation of the LGU-approved GPB.
3. Conduct gender analysis and administer the use of HGDG tool to
determine the cost to be attributed to GAD in the implementation of LGUs
major PPAs during GPB preparation.
4. Employ the use of HGDG test using PIMME checklist or FIMME
checklist to determine actual expenditure that can be attributed to the
GAD in the GAD Accomplishment Report.

13.6 We recommended, and the Management agreed, through the Municipal
Accountant, to enforce the ₱5,000.00 minimum net take home pay of the
officials and employees, as provided in Section 52 of the General Provisions of
General Appropriations Act of 2019.

We recommended that the LCE oblige: (5) The Municipal Treasurer to transfer subsequent fund transfers to the appropriate Trust Fund account in accordance with the guidelines set forth for the said downloaded funds. (6) The Municipal Accountant to strictly record subsequent fund transfers in the Trust Fund books. (7) The Municipal Engineer and the Accountant to require the contractor to post the needed warranty security. (8) The completed project to be recognized in the books of account and to submit its pertinent Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer to transfer subsequent fund transfers to the appropriate Trust Fund account in accordance with the guidelines set forth for the said downloaded funds. The Municipal Accountant to strictly record subsequent fund transfers in the Trust Fund books. The Municipal Engineer and the Accountant to require the contractor to post the needed warranty security. The completed project and the aforementioned completed project to be recorded in the books of account and to submit its pertinent Memorandum of Agreement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE oblige: (9) enforce the collection of the unpaid water bills and market rentals amounting to, ₱948,005.62 and, ₱100,500.00, respectively. (10) impose the mandated fee embodied in Section 6G.02, Article G, Chapter VI of the Municipal Revenue Code. (11) execute a Contract of Lease with the market stall owners and incorporate provisions on surcharge for late payment and interest on any unpaid tax in accordance with Sections 7A.04 and 7A.05, Article A, Chapter VII of the Municipal Revenue Code. (12) strictly enforce Section 5H.02, Article H, Chapter V and Section 6G.03, Article G, Chapter VI of the Municipal Revenue Code, which respectively provides for the time and manner of payment of water bills and market rentals. (13) the Municipal Engineer to incorporate in the Water Service Contract provisions on surcharge for late payment of water bills. (14) the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded water bills and market rental receivables in accordance with Paragraph 19 of PPSAS and effect the necessary adjustments to the Accounts Receivable, Operating Lease Receivable and Government Equity account titles. (15) maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the Municipal Waterworks System and Market.
observations.managementAction: (9) Partially Implemented (10) Implemented (11) Partially Implemented (12) Partially Implemented (13) Not Implemented (14) Not Implemented (15) Not Implemented
observations.reason: Collections from unpaid water bills and market rentals were not yet fully collected by the Municipal Treasurer. Imposition of fees was implemented. Last notice was not issued after the lapse of 15-day period provided as supplied by the Senior Bookkeeper assigned to Waterworks Office. The Municipal Engineer to incorporate in the Water Service Contract provisions on surcharge for late payment of water bills was not yet implemented; the Municipal Accountant did not recognize unrecorded water bills and market rental receivables. The Municipal Accountant communicated that BLGF advised them that the Municipality’s Water System and Market Operations do not warrant classification as Business Enterprises; hence Special Accounts for the same are no longer needed. However, no document was submitted to the Audit Team to prove such advisory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: (16) conduct a regular reconciliation of their balances so that adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be adjusted and posted in their respective books. (17) record and adjust immediately in its respective books all reconciling items found to be valid. (18) make a request/demand letter from the concerned depository banks with copies of its respective Credit Memos (CMs) and Debit Memos (DMs) to determine their validity and effects. (19) review and analyze the prior and current years’ reconciling items which causes and origins remained to be unknown up to this date and locate its pertinent supporting documents. (20) make representations to the manager of the concerned bank with regard to details on individual salary borrowers for bank reconciling items amounting to ₱102,335.77.
observations.managementAction: (16) Implemented (17) Not Implemented (18) Partially Implemented (19) Not Implemented (20) Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer conducted some reconciliations; however, adjustments were not all made. The Municipal Treasurer made inquiry to the concerned bank about the nature of CMs and DMs; the bank could not ascertain the nature of such items. The nature of some reconciling items could not be established by the Municipal Accountant due to lacking supporting documents. No copy of LCE’s letter to the concerned bank was furnished to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE oblige: (22) the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the proper charging of expenditures to the appropriate expense items provided in the Annual Budget. (23) the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of job order personnel in the appropriate MOOE account titles in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.reason: The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant adjusted charging of expenditures and recorded wages of job order personnel in the appropriate MOOE account titles as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE oblige: (24) all its Revenue Collection Clerks to remit to the Liquidating Officer and the latter to the Municipal Treasurer, and the full amount of their collections and remittances daily, regardless of the nature of collections made. (25) the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections as well as collections turned over to her by the Liquidating Officer daily or on the next banking day, particularly when the collections reach ₱10,000.00, pursuant to Section 69(1) of PD 1445 and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382. (26) the Liquidating Officer to check the accuracy of the amounts extended and the footing of the remittances made by the Revenue Collection Clerks and to verify the remaining accountable forms in the hands of the collectors to assure all collections were remitted. (27) the Municipal Treasurer to review the report of the Liquidating Officer. (28) the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries for the noted variances.
observations.reason: Collections from RCC to the Liquidating Officer and from the LO to the MT were not remitted daily in some instances, but measures were implemented: the MT deposits collections as required; the Liquidating Officer checks accuracy and footing; reports reviewed; Municipal Accountant prepared adjusting entries for noted variances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require: (29) the GFPS TWG to implement all PPAs and the programs/project/activities included in the DILG set out in its Annual Procurement Plan. Henceforth, ensure that the 20% Development Fund is promptly and fully utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.reason: Some of the 20% Development Fund was implemented; however, the CY 2019 20% DF was not fully utilized within the year. Some PPAs charged to the said fund were still on-going as of CY 2019 year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE oblige: (34) the Municipal Solid Waste Management Officer to fast track the implementation of the Closure Plan of Existing Open Dumpsite project and to submit the suspension order evidencing the stalling of its implementation. (35) the Municipal Engineer to reflect in his Quarterly Report on Government Programs, Projects and Activities the status of implementation of the aforesaid project. observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the:
1. Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System;
2. Acting Municipal General Services Officer, or in his default, the Municipal
Treasurer, to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Local
Road Inventory and Road Map to facilitate the preparation of a proper
inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s local road network system
and to prepare as well a Report on Local Road Network;
3. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Acting
Municipal General Services Officer or in his default, the Municipal
Treasurer, with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects, and thereafter;
4. Municipal Accountant to record the amount of local road networks
equivalent to the required transition provision percentage and to disclose
the total road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

2.7 We recommended that the LCE oblige:
1. The Municipal Treasurer to transfer subsequent fund transfers to the
appropriate Trust Fund account in accordance with the guidelines set forth
for the said downloaded funds;
2. The Municipal Accountant to strictly record subsequent fund transfers in
the Trust Fund books; and
3. The Municipal Accountant to record in the books of account the
aforementioned completed project and to submit its pertinent
Memorandum of Agreement; and
4. The Municipal Engineer and the Accountant to require the contractor to
post the needed warranty security.

3.12 We recommended that the LCE oblige:
1. The Municipal Treasurer to:
a) enforce the collection of the unpaid water bills and market rentals
amounting to, ₱948,005.62 and ₱100,500.00, respectively;
b) impose the mandated fee embodied in Section 6G.02, Article G, Chapter
VI of the Municipal Revenue Code;
c) execute a Contract of Lease with the market stall owners and
incorporate provisions on surcharge for late payment and interest on any
unpaid tax in accordance with Sections 7A.04 and 7A.05, Article A,
Chapter VII of the Municipal Revenue Code; and
d) strictly enforce Section 5H.02, Article H, Chapter V and Section 6G.03,
Article G, Chapter VI of the Municipal Revenue Code, which
respectively provides for the time and manner of payment of water bills
and market rentals;
2. The Municipal Engineer to incorporate in the Water Service Contract
provisions on surcharge for late payment and interest on any unpaid tax in
accordance with Sections 7A.04 and 7A.05, Article A, Chapter VII of the
Municipal Revenue Code; and
3. The Municipal Accountant to:
a) recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded water bills and market
rentals receivables in accordance with Paragraph 19 of Philippine Public
Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) and effect the necessary
adjustments to the Accounts Receivable, Operating Lease Receivable and
Government Equity account titles; and
b) maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the Municipal
Waterworks System and Market.

4.9 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to:
1. Conduct a regular reconciliation of their balances so that adjustments made
or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be
adjusted and posted in their respective books;
2. Record and adjust immediately in its respective books all reconciling items
found to be valid;
3. Make a request/demand letter from the concerned depository banks copies
of its respective Credit Memos (CMs) and Debit Memos (DMs) to determine
their validity and effect the necessary adjustments;
4. Review and analyze the prior and current years’ reconciling items which
causes and origins remained to be unknown up to this date and locate its
pertinent supporting documents; and
4.10 We also recommended that the LCE make representations to the manager of
the concerned bank with regards to the details on individual salary borrowers
for bank reconciling items amounting to ₱102,335.77.

5.5 We recommended that the LCE oblige the Municipal Accountant to file a
request for write-off of the receivable account balances which are considered
dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance to COA Circular No.
2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

6.7 We recommended that the LCE oblige:
1. The Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the proper charging of
expenditures to the expense item provided in the Annual Budget; and
2. The Municipal Accountant to record the wages of job order personnel in the
appropriate MOOE account titles in accordance with Annex B of COA
Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

7.9 We recommended that the LCE require:
1. All its Revenue Collection Clerks to remit to the Liquidating Officer and the
latter to the Municipal Treasurer, the full amount of their collections and
remittances daily, regardless of the nature of collections made;
2. The Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections as well as
collections turned over to her by the Liquidating Officer daily or on the
next banking day, particularly when the collections reach ₱10,000.00,
pursuant to Section 69(1) of PD 1445 and Section 28 of COA Circular No.
92-382;
3. The Liquidating Officer to check the accuracy of the amounts extended and
the footing of the remittances made by the Revenue Collection Clerks and
to verify the remaining accountable forms in the hands of the collectors to
assure all collections were remitted;
4. The Municipal Treasurer to review the report of the Liquidating Officer;
and
5. The Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries for the noted
variances.

8.5 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator, and all others concerned to
implement the programs/projects/activities under the 20% Development Fund
in accordance with its Schedule of Procurement Activities set out in its Annual
Procurement Plan. Henceforth, ensure that the 20% Development Fund is
promptly and fully utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2017-1.

9.10 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Transfer to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund any
subsequent unexpended prior year balance which is already due for
reversion;
2. Support subsequent disbursements charged to Quick Response Fund with
Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and the declaration of the state of calamity;
3. Transfer the amount of ₱9,421.01 to the Special Trust Fund for DRRMF
and to effect the necessary adjusting entry, i. e., a debit to the Government
Equity account and a credit to Cash in Bank account in the General Fund
for ₱9,421.01, and a debit to Cash in Bank account and a credit to Trust
Liability – DRRMF CY 2018 account in the Trust Fund for ₱9,421.01; and
4. Disclose in its Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of
the unexpended balance of its LDRRMF.

10.10 We recommended that the LCE oblige:
1. The Municipal Solid Waste Management Officer to fast track the
implementation of the Closure Plan of Existing Open Dumpsite project

2. The Municipal Engineer to reflect in his Quarterly Report on Government

3. The Municipal Budget Officer furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the
supplemental budget reflecting the appropriation for the said project.

11.7 We recommend that the LCE require:

12.6 We recommended that the LCE oblige the Municipal Accountant and

13.11 We recommended that the LCE oblige:

and

14.12 We recommended that the LCE oblige the GFPS TWG to implement all PPAs

GAD Plan and Budget should there be changes in the original GAD Plan and
Budget, henceforth, strictly observe the guidelines set forth in PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12,
2016.

15.5 We recommended that the Chief Executive require the BAC and Municipal
Accountant to oblige the concerned suppliers and contractors to post warranty
securities for the procurement of good and infrastructure projects that had
already been fully paid. Henceforth, strictly observe the mandatory posting of

Henceforth, adhere strictly to COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has yet to record the donated equipment. Moreover, require the Property and Supply unit to record the other in-kind donations in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM. Henceforth, adhere strictly to COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to record the procurement of supplies, inventory of all accountable forms, fuels, lubricants, drugs and medicines through inventory account and credit the same for issuance to end-users, transfers or other disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has yet to record the supplies, fuels, lubricants, drugs and accountable forms through inventory account and credit the same for issuance to end-users, transfers or other disposal.
observations.reason: A total of 221,325 pieces of accountable forms and supplies were not yet used or issued as of CY 2017 year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 particularly on the fund transfers. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor request the NIA administration to submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents to validate the completion of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has yet to send the request to NIA for the liquidation documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant be required to submit immediately the ten disbursement vouchers,
together with the supporting documents corresponding to the recorded
disbursements totaling ₱2,147,784.53. Henceforth, strictly observe the timely
submission of accounts pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 of
COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
adjusting entries on the stale checks to correct the Cash in Bank - Local
Currency, Current Account and Government Equity balances.

We recommended that Management limit the use of the LDRRM Fund to
disaster related activities as provided for in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012, NDRRMC-DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-1 dated March 23, 2013 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the management strictly observe the guidelines on the
appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided for
in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22,
2017.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of
Section 4(2) of PD 1445 and Section 305(b) of Republic Act 7160 and develop a
system of monitoring on the use of the financial assistance to ensure that the
funds are spent for the purpose for which it was intended. We also recommend
that the Local School Board request the recipients to submit the liquidation
report and other documents to validate the proper utilization of the funds.
Henceforth, ensure that all disbursements are properly and completely
documented.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of allowing
personnel hired through contracts of service and job orders to attend
workshops and seminars and claim travelling expenses and strictly adhere to
the above-mentioned CSC guidelines. This recommendation is without
prejudice to the subsequent issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly withhold
income taxes from its honoraria payments in accordance with Section 2.79 of
Revenue Regulation (RR) No. 2-98 as amended by Section 3 of RR 10-2008.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer
immediately remit the amount of ₱141,517.89 to GSIS. Henceforth, strictly
adhere to the provisions cited in PD 1445, COA Circular No. 92-382 and IRR of
RA No. 8291 on the remittance of GSIS premiums and loan amortizations
withheld.

We recommended that the LGU observe strictly the prescribed minimum
employee’s monthly net take home pay under Section 47 of the General
Provisions of the General Appropriations Act of 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board
actively participate in the gradual implementation of the approved 10-year
Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan to protect the constituents and the
environment against health and environmental hazards brought about by
improper solid waste disposal.

We recommended that projects be fully implemented upon receipt of the
funds for the benefit of the constituents/beneficiaries. We also recommended
that transactions involving fund transfers be supported by Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement and that management strictly observe the
submission of contracts and its supporting documents within five (5) working
days from its execution.

We recommended that management endeavor to fully implement the
programs/projects/activities contained in its GAD Plan and Budget to realize its
intended purposes. We also recommended that management adhere to the
DILG-approved GAD Plan and Budget in the implementation and utilization of
the programs and funds incorporated in the said plan and budget. Further, it is
also recommended that the GAD Focal Person coordinate with the Municipal
Accountant in accomplishing the GAD Accomplishment Report with respect to
the amounts to be presented in the said report. Furthermore, it is likewise
recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer sees to it that the amounts
presented in the Annual Budget be accurate and consistent.

We recommend that management identify, select and invite observers in all stages of procurement and properly undertake post qualification in accordance with Sections 13 and 34.3.b.ii of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: There is still no mechanism for identifying/selecting the group from which observers were invited for specific procurements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Procurement processes now identify, select and invite observers in some procurements; however, a mechanism for identifying/selecting the group from which observers were invited for specific procurements is not yet established.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that transactions involving fund transfers be supported by Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and authority by the Local Sanggunian for the Local Chief Executive to enter into contracts, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already sent a request to the Area Coordinator; still waiting for the submission of the requested documents.
observations.reason: There is pending submission of the requested MOAs and supporting documents from implementing agencies/partners.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Social Welfare Officer be required to formulate policies/guidelines on the grant of educational assistance and submit the same to the Sanggunian for approval pursuant to Section 483 (b) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Policies/guidelines on the grant of educational assistance have been submitted but not yet adopted by the Sanggunian.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to maintain a separate registries and subsidiary records for the LDRRMF and submit to COA a Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF. Likewise, require the LDRRMO to submit a LDRRM Plan.

We recommend that a full-blown information and dissemination campaign on proper solid waste management be conducted so that the Municipality’s constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the program.
observations.managementAction: Massive information campaign on proper solid waste management has already been conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal School Board prepare an Annual School Board Budget duly approved by all members of the Local School Board and duly supported with said joint circular.
observations.managementAction: The School Board has prepared its Annual School Board Budget duly approved by all members of the Local School Board and duly supported with programs aligned to the said joint circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the list and details of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented within the CY 2017.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted its quarterly list of all on-going and completed programs/projects/activities (PPAs) for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the discrepancy between physical and book inventories be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately. Also, create a committee for evaluating and creating an Inventory List.
observations.managementAction: On the process of evaluating and creating a committee.
observations.reason: Inventory list is incomplete as of CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned officials and employees be required to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012, otherwise, suspend their salaries until all cash advances are liquidated and file appropriate criminal and administrative cases as provided for in Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Disallowed or outstanding cash advances were from Non-LGU personnel, retired, terminated or resigned with unclaimed terminal benefits. The Municipal Accountant refrained from granting cash advances to officers and employees with outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: A total of ₱88,126.48 unliquidated cash advances as of Dec. 31, 2017 remain from non-LGU personnel, retired, terminated or resigned with unclaimed terminal benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management establish a GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) and ensure its functionality. Likewise, prepare GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and submit the same to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval. Moreover, ensure that the details or breakdown of the specific programs, projects and activities of each office are stated in the obligation requests, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal Point System has already been established but is not fully functional as indicated by its failure to fully implement its programs/projects/activities for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to analyze the Cash in Bank account, identify the nature of the reconciling items and adjust the books accordingly. Likewise, she should maintain subsidiary ledgers/records for all banks accounts.
observations.managementAction: Due to delayed receipt of CY 2012 BRS and on the process of enrolling to online LBP access.
observations.reason: Preparation of the 2012 BRS is delayed. Municipality is on the process of enrolling to online LBP access.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary records for receivable accounts so that appropriate steps are taken up to collect said receivables.
observations.managementAction: Required subsidiary records were prepared for receivable accounts; however, there were still receivable balances in the previous years which could not still be accounted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain in the General Fund a Special Account for the Municipality’s Waterworks Office. All employees should be enrolled under the Municipal Engineering Office. Also, study the status of Waterworks System.
observations.managementAction: Management requested to defer the implementation because the municipality has employees under the Municipal Engineering Office.
observations.reason: Currently not sustainable because of the minimal cost per cubic meter.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that officers and employees concerned liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances and strictly enforce the pertinent sanctions provided in COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Balances were paid through reimbursement. The Municipal Accountant refrained from granting cash advances to officers and employees with outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management limit the expenditures for Personal Services to 45% of the total annual income from regular sources of the next preceding fiscal year.
observations.managementAction: For strict compliance. Municipality will not create new positions.
observations.reason: Municipality still exceeded its PS limitation for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the officers and employees concerned be required to submit appropriate travel documents to audit and settle claims and refund the excess claims amounting to ₱35,040.00.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement Vouchers for said transactions were disallowed in prior audit and were settled in CY 2016 by persons liable.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity should strictly adhere with the provisions of LOI 755, Administrative Order No. 17 and Section 52 and 7.2 of the Revised IRR and Sec. 114 of the Manual for Local Government Units for procurement of common-use office supplies.
observations.managementAction: Common-use office supplies were still not procured from the Procurement Service.
observations.reason: Common-use office supplies were still not procured from the Procurement Service.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to maintain in the General Fund a Special Account for the Municipality’s Waterworks Office.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014, AAR 2013, AAR 2012 — Municipal Engineering Office; management requested to defer implementation as municipality has yet to decide and study the status of Waterworks System.
observations.reason: Management requested to defer the implementation because the municipality has yet to decide and study the status of Waterworks. Currently not sustainable because of the minimal cost per cubic meter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local
road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be substantially complied, thus,
require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the local roads
and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.
Likewise, enjoin all concerned department heads to submit a lapsing schedule
for the computation of depreciation, maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road
Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, render a Report on
Local Road Network and recognize impairment on infrastructure assets.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry
voucher to record the donated equipment to its proper asset accounts and
provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Moreover,
require the Property and Supply unit to record the other in-kind donations in
the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM. Henceforth, adhere strictly to
COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of
the LDRRMF.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to record the
procurement of supplies, accountable forms, fuels, lubricants, drugs and
medicines through inventory account and credit the same for issuance to end-
users, transfers or other disposal.

We recommend that the BAC identify, select and invite observers in all
stages of procurement and to properly undertake post qualification in
accordance with Sections 13 and 34.3.b.ii of RA 9184 and its Implementing
Rules and Regulations.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 particularly on fund transfers.

We also recommend that management requests the NIA administration to
submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents to validate the
completion of the projects and the utilization of the funds transferred to the
said Office. This recommendation is without prejudice to the result of post-
audit of the liquidation report/documents.

For similar transfer of funds in the ensuing year, see to it that the MOA shall
stipulate the period of implementation of the project, the obligation of the
Implementing Agency to submit liquidation reports, and to return any unutilized
funds.

We recommend that transactions involving fund transfers be supported
by Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and authority by the Local Sanggunian
for the Local Chief Executive to enter into contract pursuant to the
requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend that the Social Welfare and Development Officer be
required to formulate policies/guidelines on the grant of educational assistance
and submit the same to the Sangunian for approval pursuant to Section 483 (b)
of RA 7160.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer be required to
maintain a separate registries and for Municipal Accountant to subsidiary
records for the LDRRMF and submit to COA a Report on the Sources and
Utilization of LDRRMF. Likewise, require the LDRRMO to submit a LDRRM
Plan.

We recommend that a full-blown information and dissemination
campaign on proper solid waste management be conducted so that the
Municipalitys constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the
program.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board prepare an Annual
School Board Budget duly approved by all members of the Local School Board
and duly supported with programs, projects and activities that are aligned with
Sections 99 (a) and 100 (c) of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM, and DILG Joint
Circular No. 01, s. of 1998, dated April 4, 1998.

We recommend that management submit the list of all on-going
government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be
implemented during the year within the reglementary period provided under
COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
We recommend that the AAR, discrepancy between physical and book inventories be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 AAR, Finding No. 1 and AAR, Finding No. 7 CY 2012 AAR, Finding No. 4 creating a committee.
observations.reason: Inventory List is incomplete as of CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant take into account the residual value of the property in the computation of the allowance for depreciation.
We recommend the BAC strictly observe the procurement processes as outlined in the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184, as amended and submit the lacking procurement documents.

We recommend that the concerned officials and employees be required to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012; otherwise, suspend the salaries of the accountable officials and employees until all cash advances are liquidated. This is without prejudice to the filing of the appropriate criminal and administrative cases against them as provided for in Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.reason: The balance of unliquidated cash advances as of Dec. 31, 2016 were from Non-LGU personnel, retired, terminated or resigned with unclaimed Terminal Benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management establish a GAD Focal Point System and ensure its functionality. Likewise, prepare GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and submit the same to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval. Moreover, see to it that the details or breakdown of the specific programs, projects and activities of each office are stated or clearly identified in the obligation requests, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents, thus making it possible to determine whether the expenditures are aligned with the GAD Plan.
observations.reason: For full implementation will be in CY 2017. Details should be indicated in GAD DV, OR and Accomplishment Reports and submit supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to analyze the Cash in Bank account, identify the nature of the reconciling items and adjust the books accordingly. Likewise, maintain subsidiary ledgers/records for all banks accounts.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR, Finding No. 1; CY 2012 AAR, Finding No. 2 — preparation of BRS is delayed due to delayed receipt of bank statements; enrolling to online LBP access is in process.
observations.reason: Due to delayed receipt of Bank Statements, preparation of BRS is delayed. The Municipality is in the process of enrolling to online LBP access.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary records for receivable accounts so that appropriate steps are taken up in the collection of said previous receivables.
observations.reason: However, there were still balances from previous years which could not still be accounted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require officers and employees concerned to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances and to strictly enforce the pertinent sanctions provided for in COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012, including suspension of salaries if the demand remains unheeded, without prejudice to filing appropriate cases.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 6 — The Municipal Accountant refrained from granting cash advances and travelling expenses were paid through reimbursement.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has refrained from granting cash advances to employees; travelling expenses were paid through reimbursement. Some balances were settled through reimbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management limit the amount of appropriations and expenditures for personal services to 45% of the total annual income from regular sources of the next preceding fiscal year.
observations.managementAction: AAR — For strict compliance. Municipality will not create new positions.
observations.reason: For strict compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the practice of paying leave monetization benefits in excess of 30 days, unless even valid and justifiable reasons for such grant are submitted.

We recommend that the officers and employees concerned be required to submit appropriate travel documents to support said claims and to refund the excess claims for travel expenses totaling P35,040.00.
observations.reason: Disbursement Vouchers for said transactions were disallowed in audit and were settled in CY 2016 by persons liable.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer secure approval from the Department of Finance if they continue to maintain their depository account with the PNB, in pursuance with the provision of DOF Department Order No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005.

We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity require the BAC to strictly adhere with the provisions of LOI 755, Administrative Order No. 17 and Section 52 and 7.2 of the Revised IRR and Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for its procurement of common use office supplies.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 8 — Direct payments are made to suppliers.
observations.reason: For CY 2012 compliance. Direct payments are made to suppliers.
